skill.name: pitfalls-drizzle-orm
skill.description: Guides Drizzle ORM schema work, migration safety checks, type inference patterns, array column syntax, and validation setup.
skill.summary: Avoid common Drizzle ORM schema, migration, and type inference mistakes.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance findings are mostly false positives caused by Markdown code fences and database terminology. The only meaningful concern is operational: the skill documents a forced Drizzle migration command that can cause data loss if copied without review.

medium_findings.title: Forced Migration Command Can Cause Data Loss
medium_findings.description: The skill shows npm run db:push --force after a data-loss warning. The surrounding guidance warns against production use without backup, so this is an operational risk rather than malicious behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command and warning are directly present in the Markdown. The risk is limited because the file describes manual usage and includes a backup warning.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby/shell backtick findings point to Markdown code fences and examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. No file in the skill automatically executes these snippets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown fence markers around examples. There is no script file, interpreter invocation, or automatic command execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography finding points to the frontmatter description. That line discusses Drizzle triggers and type inference, not cryptographic algorithms or crypto APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only descriptive metadata for Drizzle ORM usage. No cryptographic primitive, hashing function, cipher, or key handling appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The static reconnaissance finding points to a database migration warning about changing primary key types. It does not enumerate the host, network, users, files, or environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is a comment about schema type migration risk. It contains no command or instruction that gathers system information.

dangerous_patterns.title: Manual Forced Migration Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The command npm run db:push --force can bypass data-loss prompts. It is documented as manual guidance and includes a production backup warning.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly documented in the skill. It is not executed by the skill, but it can be dangerous if used on production data.

content.value_statement: Drizzle ORM schema changes can break migrations or weaken type safety. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code review common Drizzle patterns before changes reach production.
actual_capabilities: Checks Drizzle schema definitions for common migration pitfalls.
Flags primary key type changes before production migration work.
Verifies correct array column syntax for text arrays.
Prompts use of $inferSelect and $inferInsert type patterns.
Recommends drizzle-zod validation for insert and select schemas.
Provides a concise migration safety checklist.
limitations: Does not inspect a live database or migration history.
Does not replace database backups or migration reviews.
Does not validate every Drizzle dialect or adapter behavior.
Includes manual command examples that require human approval.

use_cases.title: Review a New Schema
use_cases.description: Check table definitions for safe primary keys, array syntax, and exported model types.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Migration
use_cases.description: Review a planned Drizzle migration before running db:push in development or staging.

use_cases.title: Improve Type Safety
use_cases.description: Add inferred select and insert types plus drizzle-zod validation to existing models.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Schema
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one Drizzle table.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Drizzle schema for common pitfalls. Focus on primary keys, array columns, inferred types, and validation setup.

prompt_templates.title: Review Migration Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Drizzle schema changes before I run a migration. Identify possible data loss, primary key changes, and backup requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Add Type Inference
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this Drizzle schema plan to include safe $inferSelect and $inferInsert exports for each model.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Drizzle Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Drizzle ORM implementation for schema design pitfalls, migration hazards, array syntax issues, and missing drizzle-zod validation.

output_examples.input: A table definition changes an id column from serial to uuid.
output_examples.output: The review flags the primary key type change as unsafe for production migration. It recommends a planned migration path and backup first.

output_examples.input: A schema uses an array helper around a text column.
output_examples.output: The review explains that Drizzle text arrays should use the column array method and asks for a syntax correction.

output_examples.input: A model has no exported insert or select types.
output_examples.output: The review recommends exported inferred types and matching drizzle-zod schemas for validation.

best_practices: Run migration checks in development or staging before production.
Review every primary key change as a high-risk database change.
Pair inferred Drizzle types with drizzle-zod validation where inputs enter the system.
anti_patterns: Changing production primary key types without a migration plan.
Using forced migration commands without confirming backups.
Defining schemas without exported insert and select types.

faq.question: Does this skill run database commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and command examples. Users must decide whether to run any command.

faq.question: Can it prevent all migration failures?
faq.answer: No. It catches common Drizzle pitfalls, but real migrations still need testing and backups.

faq.question: Why are primary key type changes highlighted?
faq.answer: Primary key type changes can break migrations, references, and existing production data.

faq.question: Does it support drizzle-zod?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends drizzle-zod patterns for insert and select validation schemas.

faq.question: Is this useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Should I use db:push --force in production?
faq.answer: Only after expert review, backups, and a clear recovery plan. The skill warns against casual production use.
